summary: Solid 1985 soundboard capture with a smoking China Cat > I Know You Rider and strong Comes‑A‑Time/Midnight Hour performances.
review: The recording is a clean SBD that, while not the absolute pinnacle of ’85 tape quality, sounds clear enough to enjoy the band’s tight playing. Highlights include an electrifying China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a heartfelt Comes A Time and a joyous Midnight Hour encore; even the notoriously fast Stagger Lee lands with gusto. Minor drawbacks are a few vocal hiccups on Dupree’s and a lukewarm take on He’s Gone.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars, praising both performance and soundboard quality; only one outlier low rating drags the simple average down, but the weighted assessment remains strongly positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and well‑balanced SBD; a few reviewers note it isn’t “pristine” by peak ’85 standards but overall clear with no major dropouts.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely with upbeat numbers and a solid ballad flow; the second set kicks off strong with China Cat > Rider and maintains momentum through Comes A Time, while the encore delivers an uplifting Midnight Hour.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strong guitar work on Dupree's and Stagger Lee; occasional vocal strain noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Funky bass intro into China Cat; heavy‑duty bomb bursts in mid‑set praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Slide work highlighted on Stagger Lee; overall solid rhythm contributions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards shine on Don't Ease and the vocal combo early in the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Big drums get “dusted off” with powerful space sections and an energetic encore.
